text: Evaluating the similarity between various units of text—from individual words to entire documents—is a foundational requirement for numerous Natural Language Processing (NLP) applications. These include information retrieval, document clustering, machine translation, and automated grading systems for essays and short answers. This survey provides a comprehensive overview of existing text similarity research by categorizing methods into three primary frameworks: String-based, Corpus-based, and Knowledge-based approaches. String-based measures focus on lexical patterns and character sequences, while Corpus-based and Knowledge-based techniques leverage large-scale datasets and semantic networks to capture deeper meaning. Additionally, this paper explores the growing trend of hybrid models that integrate multiple similarity metrics to achieve higher performance and more robust results.
